compiled_state.title: Update review-fix-dispatch.md triage routing for fragile severity
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
Update plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/review-fix-dispatch.md triage table:
1. Add fragile alongside important and critical for Fix/Defend routing
2. Add explicit note: fragile findings NEVER go to Defer — always Fix or Defend
3. Update the Minor findings note (lines 80-83) to clarify fragile is NOT minor and cannot be Deferred

Note: Done definition 4 (resolution triage routes fragile to Fix or Defend only) is enforced at the prompt guidance level, not by code. The review resolution agent reads this file as instructions. There is no runtime code path that programmatically prevents deferring a fragile finding — enforcement depends on the agent following the triage table. This is consistent with how important/critical routing works (also prompt-guided).

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/review-fix-dispatch.md (modify)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test suite passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] fragile mentioned in Fix/Defend routing section
  Verify: grep -q 'fragile' plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/review-fix-dispatch.md
- [ ] fragile explicitly excluded from Defer
  Verify: grep -qi 'fragile.*never.*defer\|fragile.*not.*defer' plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/review-fix-dispatch.md

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — Acceptance criteria verified: (1) fragile present in Fix/Defend routing section, (2) fragile explicitly excluded from Defer with 'NEVER go to Defer' language
